mission.title: thanos: thanos+ingress-nginx+grpc: impossible setup due missing host header
mission.description: thanos+ingress-nginx+grpc: impossible setup due missing host header. This issue affects 12+ users.

steps.title: Identify thanos deploy symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your thanos deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=thanos
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `2 while reading response header from upstream, client: 172.26.81.74, server: sidecar-k8s-live-a.ops.example.com, request`

steps.title: Check current Service configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant thanos resources:
```bash
kubectl get service -A
kubectl describe service <name> -n <namespace>
```
**Thanos, Prometheus and Golang version used**
quay.io/thanos/thanos:v0.7.0

**What happened**
i setup 2 kubernetes clusters, thanos query is in one cluster (and a local prometheus+sidecar) and need t

steps.title: Apply the fix for thanos+ingress-nginx+grpc: impossible setup due missing host
steps.description:
To avoid needing a query per remote cluster, get the name to add to
the dial options from the dns provider when making the grpc connection.
```yaml
---
apiVersion: extensions/v1beta1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: monitoring-ingress
  namespace: monitoring
  annotations:
    kubernetes.io/ingress.class: "nginx"
spec:
  rules:
  - host: prometheus-k8s-live-a.ops.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        backend:
          serviceName: prometheus-k8s-live-a
          servicePort: 9090
  - host: prometheus-k8s-live-b.ops.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        backend:
          serviceName: prometheus-k8s-live-b
          servicePort: 9090
  tls:
  - hosts:
    - prometheus-k8s-
```

steps.title: Confirm thanos+ingress-nginx+grpc: impossible setup due mi is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `2 while reading response header from upstream, client: 172.26.81.74, server: sidecar-k8s-live-a.ops.example.com, request` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: To avoid needing a query per remote cluster, get the name to add to
the dial options from the dns provider when making the grpc connection.. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with thanos installed or the issue environment reproducible.
